question: A quantitative trading firm deploys proprietary algorithms on a shared hybrid cloud infrastructure (AWS and on-prem). Design a confidential computing architecture using AMD SEV-SNP or Intel TDX that protects algorithms from privileged insiders, cloud administrators, and co-tenant VMs. Detail the attestation flow, key sealing strategy, memory isolation, and algorithm update mechanism without exposing plaintext models.
answer: Deploy AMD SEV-SNP or Intel TDX confidential VMs for algorithm execution. A hardware attestation service issues reports verified by orchestration before releasing keys from Vault with a PKCS#11 HSM. Algorithms use envelope encryption with KEK in HSM and DEKs sealed to VM attestation. The orchestrator verifies firmware, TCB, and measurement before decryption. SEV-SNP RMP tables prevent host memory inspection. KEKs rotate per NIST SP 800-57 every 90 days and DEKs per deployment.
diagram:
flowchart TD
  O[Orchestrator] -->|1. Launch VM| H[Hypervisor]
  H -->|2. SEV-SNP VM| V[Confidential VM]
  V -->|3. Request attestation| AS[Attestation Service]
  AS -->|4. Signed report| V
  V -->|5. Forward report| O
  O -->|6. Verify measurement| O
  O -->|7. Release algorithm| V
  V -->|8. Request DEK unwrap| KMS[KMS + HSM]
  KMS -->|9. Return DEK| V
  V -->|10. Decrypt & execute| R[Algorithm output]
  R -->|11. Sign result| O
